zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh Lambert於1339年出生在英國越可爾時，他的父親Randulphus Lambert22歲，母親Alainare deToni18歲。他與Maud De Ros結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh Lambert于1339年出生在英国越可爾时，他的父亲Randulphus Lambert22岁，母亲Alainare deToni18岁。他与Maud De Ros结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, German, Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In England, the native Old English form Landbeorht was replaced by Lambert, the Continental form of the name that was taken to England by the Normans from France. The name gained wider currency in Britain in the Middle Ages with the immigration of weavers from Flanders, among whom Saint Lambert or Lamprecht, bishop of Maastricht in around 700, was a popular cult figure. In Italy the name was popularized in the Middle Ages as a result of the fame of Lambert I and II, Dukes of Spoleto and Holy Roman Emperors. Lambert is the second most frequent surname in Wallonia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht (see also Lampert ).

History: Aubin Lambert dit Champagne from Tourouvre in Orne, France, married Élisabeth Aubert in Quebec City, QC, in 1670; see also Champagne . Eustache Lambert from Boulogne-sur-Mer in Pas-de-Calais, France, married Marie Laurence in France c. 1656, died in Quebec City, QC, in 1673. Pierre Lambert from Fourmetot in Eure, France, married Marie Normand in Quebec City, QC, in 1680. Augustin Hébert dit Lambert, a descendant of Augustin Hébert from Paris, France, married Françoise Petit in Varennes, QC, in 1761. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Lambers
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberth
meanings: English: variant of   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lambertus
meanings: German and Dutch: humanistic name, Latinized form of the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gaetano, Luciano, Amedeo, Angelo, Biagio, Domenic, Donato, Enrico, Gino, Iolanda.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Lamberto, an equivalent of Lambert . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.
German and Dutch: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  , using the genitive of its Latinized form   Lambertus  .

variants.name: Lamberty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ana, Dimas, Jose, Juan, Altagracia, Angel, Blanca, Efrain, Fernando, Jaime, Ramon.
German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Lambert , from Lamberti, the genitive of its Latinized form Lambertus . Compare Lamberti 2.

variants.name: Lambertz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Erna, Johann, Ralf.
North German: patronymic from   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberto
meanings: Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Lamberto (see Lambert ).

variants.name: Lampert
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, and Slovak; Hungarian (Lampért): variant of Lambert . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed some cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht.
English: variant of Lambard, an occupational name from Middle English lambherd, lambhird, lambhurd ‘lamb-herd’ (Old English lamb + hierde).

variants.name: Lambertsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Dutch, Danish, North German, and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  . This surname has died out in the Netherlands.
History: Cornelius Lambertsen came from Utrecht, the Netherlands, arriving at New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1642.

variants.name: Lamb
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Lamb, a pet form of Lambert .
English: nickname for a meek and inoffensive person, from Middle English lamb, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of lambs. See also Lamm .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luain ‘descendant of the warrior’, formerly Anglicized as O'Loan (see Lane 3). MacLysaght comments: "The form Lamb(e), which results from a more than usually absurd pseudo-translation (uan ‘lamb’), is now much more numerous than O'Loan itself.".

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh Lambert was born in 1339, in Yorkshire, England, his father, Randulphus Lambert, was 22 and his mother, Alainare deToni, was 18. He married Maud De Ros. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh Lambert est né en 1339, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Randulphus Lambert, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Alainare deToni, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Maud De Ros. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh Lambert 1339 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Randulphus Lambert 22 und seine Mutter Alainare deToni 18. Er hat Maud De Ros geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Lambert è nato il nel 1339 nel Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Randulphus Lambert, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Alainare deToni, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Maud De Ros. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Cravins, Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Lambertが1339年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Randulphus Lambertは22歳，母Alainare deToniは18歳であった。Maud De Rosと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Lambert(이)가 1339년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Randulphus Lambert(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Alainare deToni(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Maud De Ros(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh Lambert nasceu em 1339, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Randulphus Lambert, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Alainare deToni, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud De Ros. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh Lambert родился в 1339 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Randulphus Lambert было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Alainare deToni, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud De Ros. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh Lambert nació en 1339, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Randulphus Lambert, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Alainare deToni, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Maud De Ros. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
